experiments.title: Geometric Volatility Index
experiments.summary: 1 Hz team-shape volatility built from spread, team length, inter-team centroid distance, and spatial entropy.
experiments.interpretation: Higher values indicate rapid spatial reorganization and a more unstable game state.

metrics.label: Peak vol index
metrics.hint: Highest normalized combined volatility over the half.

metrics.label: Sampling
metrics.hint: Per-second series with 10-second rolling mean absolute deltas.

metrics.label: Goals in clip
metrics.hint: Count of SoccerNet goal annotations aligned to this half.

metrics.label: 30s pre-goal index
metrics.hint: Average combined volatility index in rows that precede a goal within 30 seconds.

metrics.label: 30s baseline index
metrics.hint: Average combined volatility index outside those 30-second pre-goal windows.

metrics.label: 30s uplift
metrics.hint: Relative lift of the volatility index in pre-goal windows versus baseline.

diagnostics.title: Field map stayed online
diagnostics.message: Field registration covered 2708 player points with a 0.9952 registered ratio. Calibration refresh succeeded 19 times out of 25 attempts (0.76), and the last good calibration was at frame 240 of 250.
diagnostics.next_step: Use projected player coordinates for this clip, but inspect frames 241-249 for any late calibration drift before exporting.

diagnostics.title: Track stitching reduced ID churn
diagnostics.message: Post-merge player IDs dropped to 23 from 34 raw IDs, with 11 merges applied and a stitched ID reduction of 0.323529. Average track length increased to 118.3043 frames from a raw 80.0294, while the longest track held the full 250 frames.
diagnostics.next_step: Keep hybrid ReID stitching enabled for similar clips and review the 11 merge decisions if identity precision matters more than continuity.

diagnostics.title: Ball tracking is sparse
diagnostics.message: Only 19 ball rows were produced across 250 frames, or 0.076 detections per frame. There is just 1 ball track ID, but coverage is too thin to trust ball-driven events or possession sequences in this run.
diagnostics.next_step: Re-run with a clip where the ball is larger or less occluded, and verify ball detector settings before using any ball-derived outputs.

diagnostics.title: Team counts are imbalanced
diagnostics.message: Assigned player tracks split 17 home to 6 away, with 0 unassigned. Average player detections were 10.884 per frame, so the clip likely does not contain full 22-player visibility, and the team split is too uneven for lineup-level interpretation.
diagnostics.next_step: Check camera framing and occlusion in this segment, and avoid using this run for team-count or formation conclusions without manual review.

diagnostics.title: Experiment is not goal-aligned
diagnostics.message: The only experiment is geometric volatility, but goal_aligned_experiment is false and goal_events_count is 0. Its pre-goal and baseline values are all 0.0, so this run does not test whether the signal separates goal phases.
diagnostics.next_step: Do not use the volatility output for goal-related decisions on this clip; run it on footage with aligned goal annotations or switch to non-goal evaluation criteria.

learn_cards.title: 1. Player detection + hybrid ReID tracker
learn_cards.what_it_does: Uses football-specific detection, sparse appearance embeddings, field-aware association, and a stitch pass so player IDs can survive short occlusions and re-entries.
learn_cards.what_breaks: Crowded same-kit players, close-up broadcast cuts, and tiny distant crops can still create duplicate IDs.
learn_cards.what_to_try_next: Inspect raw-vs-stitched ID counts and longest tracks before trusting any per-player sequence.

learn_cards.title: 2. Team ID from jersey colors
learn_cards.what_it_does: Samples upper-body jersey colors, clusters them into two groups, and tags tracks as home or away.
learn_cards.what_breaks: Goalkeepers, referees, shadows, and tiny crops can poison the color signal.
learn_cards.what_to_try_next: Use clips with clear kit contrast first and treat the labels as approximate, not gospel.

learn_cards.title: 3. Ball tracking
learn_cards.what_it_does: Runs a second detector for the ball so the demo is about play context, not just player boxes.
learn_cards.what_breaks: The ball is small, fast, and happy to hide behind players or advertising boards.
learn_cards.what_to_try_next: Keep the detector lightweight for now and judge it by overlay sanity, not by perfection.

learn_cards.title: 4. Automatic field registration
learn_cards.what_it_does: Refreshes pitch calibration from field keypoints every 10 frames so the minimap can follow a moving broadcast camera.
learn_cards.what_breaks: If the keypoint model loses field structure during a fast cut or tight zoom, the projection has to coast on the last good calibration.
learn_cards.what_to_try_next: Watch the live preview for calibration dropouts instead of trusting the minimap blindly.

learn_cards.title: 5. Demo first, fancy later
learn_cards.what_it_does: Packages detection, appearance-aware tracking, team ID, and rough spatial context into one football-first pipeline.
learn_cards.what_breaks: Long match-wide identity is still harder than short tactical windows, especially across cutaways and resets.
learn_cards.what_to_try_next: Treat stitched IDs as tactical tracklets first, then add jersey numbers or shot segmentation if match-long identity still matters.

heuristic_diagnostics.title: Identity stitching
heuristic_diagnostics.message: Player tracker ran in hybrid_reid mode with torchvision_resnet18_imagenet@mps. Raw track IDs dropped from 34 to 23 after 11 accepted merges.
heuristic_diagnostics.next_step: Inspect long same-kit occlusions and restart phases to verify the stitched IDs stay on the same players.

heuristic_diagnostics.title: Tracking stability: acceptable
heuristic_diagnostics.message: 23 stitched player track IDs across 250 frames (raw 34, longest 250, mean 118.3).
heuristic_diagnostics.next_step: Review the longest tracks through pans and occlusions to confirm IDs stay attached to the same players.

heuristic_diagnostics.title: Team clustering: separated
heuristic_diagnostics.message: 17 home tracks and 6 away tracks from 2716 jersey crops (cluster distance 0.189, mean vote ratio 0.89).
heuristic_diagnostics.next_step: Cross-check goalkeepers, referees, and edge cases before treating the home and away labels as clean.

heuristic_diagnostics.title: Ball detections: sparse
heuristic_diagnostics.message: Average ball detections per frame 0.08; projected ball anchors 19.
heuristic_diagnostics.next_step: Treat ball output as context only and inspect the overlay for missed frames before using it analytically.

heuristic_diagnostics.title: Field calibration: active
heuristic_diagnostics.message: 19/25 refreshes succeeded; mean visible pitch keypoints 4.9; 99.5% of player detections projected.
heuristic_diagnostics.next_step: If the minimap drifts, inspect live preview frames for missing pitch keypoints before changing tracking settings.

heuristic_diagnostics.title: Goal labels: missing
heuristic_diagnostics.message: No aligned SoccerNet goal events were found for this clip.
heuristic_diagnostics.next_step: Use a clip with Labels-v2.json available if you want to evaluate the example experiment against goals.

diagnostics_summary_line: Player tracking and field projection held through most of the 250-frame clip, but ball coverage was very low and team balance is skewed toward home tracks.
